summary: I was born in Altus, Oklahoma on June 6, 1940. It's where my life began before moving around due to my mother's career. Altus holds the memory of my early days before my family circumstances changed.

My mother, Lola May Mark-Moore, was born and raised in Chickasha, Oklahoma. She was very outgoing and independent, and she was the first woman manager at Dresser Industries. She worked for Manning, Maxwell and Moore in Tulsa before moving to Pineville, Louisiana when the company was sold to Dresser.

worked at Manning, Maxwell and Moore

summary: Both my mother and father were from Chickasha, Oklahoma, where they met and married. I spent many summers there with my grandmother, who lived there for the rest of her life. Chickasha was an important place in my family's history and my childhood.

My mother was the first woman manager at Dresser Industries after they acquired Manning, Maxwell and Moore. This required her to move from Tulsa to Pineville, Louisiana. Dresser Industries was a significant part of her career and our family's relocation.

My mother worked for Manning, Maxwell and Moore in Tulsa before the company was sold to Dresser Industries. Her role there was important, and the sale led to her moving to Pineville, Louisiana. This company was a key part of her professional life.

When Manning, Maxwell and Moore was sold to Dresser Industries, my mother had to move from Tulsa to Pineville, Louisiana for her new role. This move was a significant change for our family tied to her career advancement.

My father, Gordon Talmadge Moore, was born in Chickasha, Oklahoma and had a rather dull personality compared to my mother. He worked various jobs including for Mistletoe Express and a newspaper, and later as a taxi driver in Wichita, Kansas. Tragically, he was murdered during a robbery while driving a taxi after retirement.
My father moved to Wichita, Kansas after retirement and worked as a taxi driver. Unfortunately, it was there that he was robbed and killed. Wichita marks the sad end of his life.
I started my education at Whittier Elementary School. It was the first step in my schooling before moving on to junior high and high school in Tulsa. Whittier was where my academic journey began.
After Whittier, I attended Cleveland Junior High School as part of my progression through Tulsa's school system. It was the middle step before high school and an important part of my education.

I went to Will Rogers High School in Tulsa, even though I lived in the Central High School district. My mother changed my address so I could attend Will Rogers, which was closer and more convenient. I graduated from there and was later inducted into its Hall of Fame. Many notable people, including Anita Bryant and Leon Russell, also attended Will Rogers.

Although I lived in the Central High School district in Tulsa, I did not attend Central. My mother arranged for me to go to Will Rogers High School instead. Central was the school I was zoned for but did not attend.

Anita Bryant was my best friend from sophomore year at Will Rogers High School. We bonded because neither of us came from money and were somewhat outsiders at school. She was a singer and later became famous as a 2nd runner-up Miss America and spokesperson for orange juice. We remained close over the years, and I was there for her when her sister died and was buried in Tulsa.
Anita Bryant lived in Texas later in life, but when her sister died, she came back to Tulsa for the burial. Texas was where her family resided away from Tulsa.

After graduating from Will Rogers, Anita Bryant appeared on Don McNeill's Breakfast Club, a well-known ABC radio show. This helped launch her singing career early on. It was a significant opportunity for her right out of high school.
Anita Bryant was once engaged to Pat Boone's brother after high school. This connection was part of her early life before she moved on to other opportunities in Florida and beyond.

Bob Green was Anita Bryant's husband, but he was not a good partner. I remember him making faces at her during a performance at Will Rogers, showing his ego. Despite his behavior, Anita continued her career and traveled with Bob Hope entertaining troops.
Anita Bryant traveled overseas with Bob Hope, performing for the troops. He was very kind to her, and those tours were wonderful experiences. It was a positive memory connected to her career.

Leon Russell was a year behind me at Will Rogers High School. He was quiet and nerdy looking then, very different from the famous musician he became. We didn't put him in the school's Hall of Fame for years because of his drug problems, but eventually he was inducted after he became famous.

Don Chandler was a football player from Will Rogers who went on to play in the NFL for the New York Giants and Green Bay Packers. He was the kicker who won the first two Super Bowls. He is one of the notable athletes from our school.

Don Chandler, a Will Rogers graduate, played for the New York Giants as a kicker. He was part of the early NFL history and contributed to their success. The Giants were one of the teams connected to my school's famous athletes.

Don Chandler also played for the Green Bay Packers and was the kicker for the first two Super Bowl wins. This team was part of his professional football career after graduating from Will Rogers High School.
Don Chandler, from Will Rogers High School, was the kicker who won the first two Super Bowls with the Green Bay Packers. This event is a highlight in the sports history connected to my school.

David Gates, who was in my class at Will Rogers, was known for his music even in high school. He was part of the band Bread and famous for songs like 'Baby I'm-a Want You.' We were friends, and I remember his girlfriend Jo who went to Central High School.
David Gates was the lead singer of Bread, a band famous for hits like 'Baby I'm-a Want You.' As a classmate and friend, I knew him during his early years before he became famous with Bread.

Jo was David Gates' girlfriend in high school and went to Central High School. I knew them both and remember the song 'Jo Baby' that David wrote. She has been very sick in recent years, and David has been taking care of her in California.

Paul Davis was another notable musician from Will Rogers High School. He was known for designing covers for Rolling Stone magazine as well. He was part of the talented group of alumni from our school.
Paul Davis, a Will Rogers graduate, designed covers for Rolling Stone magazine. This connection highlights the artistic talent that came from our school.
Archie Goodwin, a comic book writer, was among the notable people from Will Rogers High School. He contributed to the arts and was part of our school's impressive alumni roster.

S. E. Hinton, the author of The Outsiders, was a quiet but very nice student at Will Rogers High School. She was in the first Hall of Fame class and was known for her writing, including poetry. She was a respected figure among us.
The Outsiders, written by S. E. Hinton, is a famous novel connected to my high school through its author. It represents the literary talent that came from Will Rogers High School.

I interviewed Russell B. Myers, the creator of Broom-Hilda, who won the National Cartoonists Society Best Humor Award. He is a notable figure in cartooning and humor, connected to the broader world of arts and entertainment.
Broom-Hilda is a comic strip created by Russell B. Myers, whom I interviewed. It is well-known in the cartooning community and earned him the National Cartoonists Society Best Humor Award.
The National Cartoonists Society awarded Russell B. Myers the Best Humor Award for his work on Broom-Hilda. This organization recognizes excellence in cartooning and humor.

David Rader was a star athlete at the University of Tulsa and later served in the Oklahoma Senate. He is also a Will Rogers alumnus and was involved in selecting Hall of Fame inductees. His career spans sports and politics.
Both David Rader and Roger Randall, who went to Will Rogers, served in the Oklahoma Senate. This shows the political involvement of some of my school's alumni.

Roger Randall, a Will Rogers graduate, served in the Oklahoma Senate and was also mayor of Tulsa. He was involved in our school's Hall of Fame selection process. He is a notable figure in local politics.
Roger Randall, who attended Will Rogers High School, served as mayor of Tulsa. His leadership role is part of the legacy of our school's alumni in public service.
Gaylord Sartain was younger than me but always funny and part of the Will Rogers community. He is remembered fondly as a humorous presence among our alumni.
Paula Unruh was a senior executive in the Reagan administration and also a graduate of Will Rogers High School. She represents the high level of achievement among our school's alumni.
Paula Unruh, a Will Rogers graduate, served as a senior executive in the Reagan administration. This connects my school to national government leadership during that era.

Tulsa, Oklahoma is where I grew up and attended school, including Whittier Elementary, Cleveland Junior High, and Will Rogers High School. It is the city where many of my family events and friendships took place. Tulsa has been central to my life and memories.
